channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: Two US Troops Missing During African Lion Military Exercise in Morocco
item.description: Two U.S. service members participating in the African Lion joint military exercises were reported missing near Tan Tan in southern Morocco. AFRICOM and Moroccan forces are conducting search operations.

item.title: Trump Administration Halts US Wind Farm Projects Citing National Security
item.description: President Trump's administration has brought U.S. onshore wind development to a halt, citing national security concerns. The Financial Times reports the move affects multiple pending projects.

item.title: Israel Court Extends Detention of Gaza Flotilla Activists
item.description: An Israeli court extended by two days the detention of two activists arrested aboard a Gaza-bound flotilla intercepted by Israeli forces in international waters near Greece.

item.title: ECB Policymaker Warns Euro Zone Recession Concerns Are Real and Justified
item.description: Bank of Greece governor Yannis Stournaras warned that concerns the euro zone could slip into recession if the Middle East conflict continues are "real and justified," with Iran war talks key for ECB policy.

item.title: ADB Launches Finance Scheme for Critical Mineral Supply Chains in Asia-Pacific
item.description: The Asian Development Bank launched a new financing scheme to help countries in Asia and the Pacific develop supply chains for critical minerals used in clean energy, batteries, electric vehicles, and digital technology.

item.title: Top Senate Armed Services Democrat: No timeouts on Iran war powers
item.description: Sen. Jack Reed (D-RI) criticized Trump on the 60-day War Powers deadline, stating there are no timeouts in constitutional war powers requirements as the administration continues military action against Iran.

item.title: House Democrat calls Germany troop withdrawal no way to run foreign policy
item.description: Rep. Jason Crow (D-CO) criticized the Trump administration decision to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany, calling it a strategic mistake amid rising European security concerns.

item.title: Spanish PMs plane makes emergency landing in Turkey due to technical fault
item.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez official aircraft en route to Armenia made an emergency landing in the Turkish capital following a minor malfunction. The incident highlights ongoing diplomatic travel to the Caucasus region.

item.title: Hong Kong IPO pipeline undeterred by Iran war market volatility
item.description: The Hong Kong Stock Exchange continues its listing boom driven by AI and tech companies despite global market volatility from the Iran war. IPO bankers elsewhere are struggling while Hong Kong remains resilient.

item.title: Nobel Laureate Narges Mohammadi in Critical Condition in Iranian Hospital
item.description: Jailed Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi was moved to a cardiac care unit in northwestern Iran and remains in critical condition, her family foundation reports.

item.title: Asian Development Bank Offers Aid to Pacific Nations Hit by Iran War
item.description: The Asian Development Bank is offering financial assistance to Pacific island nations affected by economic fallout from the Iran war and oil price disruptions.

item.title: German Chancellor Downplays Rift with Trump After US Troop Drawdown
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz sought to minimize tensions with Washington after the Trump administration announced plans to withdraw 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany.

item.title: Poland Sees No Delays in US Patriot Deliveries Despite Global Pressures
item.description: Poland has not received signals that U.S. weapons deliveries including Patriot systems could be delayed, Defence Minister Wladyslaw Kosiniak-Kamysz said amid global supply chain concerns.

item.title: Gulf Stock Markets Rise as Iran-US Uncertainty Caps Upside
item.description: Gulf stock exchanges closed higher as regional investors digest ongoing uncertainty from the Iran-US conflict, though gains remained limited by geopolitical risk.

item.title: Ship Attacked Off Iran as Tehran Reviews US Response to Peace Proposal
item.description: A bulk carrier reported being attacked by multiple small craft while transiting near Iran's Sirik, the UK Maritime Trade Operations agency said, amid continued threats to shipping in the region.

item.title: Sweden Seizes Suspected False-Flag Tanker in Baltic Sea
item.description: Swedish coast guard seized a tanker suspected of operating under false flags in the Baltic Sea, amid growing concerns about shadow fleet vessels in the region.

item.title: Slovakia PM Fico to Attend Moscow Victory Day Parade
item.description: Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico will attend Russia's Victory Day parade in Moscow on May 9, Russian state television reported, in a move that could strain EU unity on Russia policy.

item.title: Hezbollah Pays Steep Price in Battle to Reverse Fortunes
item.description: Israel has displaced hundreds of thousands of Hezbollah's Shi'ite constituents and killed as many as several thousand of its fighters as domestic rivals harden their opposition against its status as an armed group.

item.title: Merz's Tussle With Trump Comes at Critical Moment for Europe
item.description: Friedrich Merz's ill-timed confrontation with Donald Trump over U.S. troop levels in Germany highlights the difficulty for European leaders navigating the new transatlantic relationship.

item.title: Zelensky arrives in Yerevan for European Political Community Summit, first visit to Armenia
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky arrived in Yerevan for the European Political Community summit, marking his first-ever visit to Armenia amid ongoing efforts to build international support against Russia.

item.title: Ukraine strikes Russian Kalibr missile carrier and patrol boat in Leningrad Oblast
item.description: Ukrainian forces struck a Russian Kalibr missile carrier and patrol boat in Leningrad Oblast, President Zelensky announced, in a significant attack on Russian naval assets far from the front lines.

item.title: China's massive stealth flying wing drones spotted together at secretive test base
item.description: Satellite imagery reveals China has deployed multiple massive stealth flying wing drones together at a secretive test base, showcasing advances in unmanned aerial vehicle technology.

item.title: Ukraine Strikes Primorsk Port and Shadow Fleet Tankers in Major Drone Offensive
item.description: Ukraine launched a wave of drone strikes targeting Russia's Primorsk port and shadow fleet tankers, part of an ongoing campaign against Russian energy infrastructure and sanctions evasion.

item.title: IAEA Reports Drone Strike Near Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Radiation Lab
item.description: The IAEA reported a drone targeted the external radiation monitoring laboratory at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, highlighting continued dangers around the facility in the conflict zone.

item.title: Ukraine Offers Finland Drone Technology Deal, Zelenskiy Announces
item.description: Ukraine has offered Finland a drone technology cooperation deal, President Volodymyr Zelenskiy announced, as Kyiv seeks to strengthen defense partnerships with Nordic countries.
